name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Co-Educational Professional Business Fraternity

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: AIChE is an organization dedicated to promoting the chemical engineering field through social, community, and professional activities. AIChE establishes business contacts to help bridge the gap between college and industry.

name: Clemson University ACM Student Chapter
summary: The professional organization for Computer Science.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
summary: BAP is an honorary organization for Accounting and Finance students and professionals. BAP promotes the study and practice of accounting, finance and information systems; providing opportunities for self-development and service

name: Genetics and Biochemistry Club
summary: Organization for undergraduate students interested in the fields of Biochemistry and/or Genetics. Undergraduates are introduced to these professional fields by faculty guest speakers, field trips, and networking with other students pursuing research.

name: Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association
summary: The Clemson University Department of Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association (BioSci GSA) is a free organization composed of all students actively pursuing M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in biological science, environmental toxicology, and microbiology.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
summary: The premier organization advancing cultural, academic, and professional interests of graduate students of African descent. Our goal is to provide a network of critical resources to ensure the academic success of emerging Black Scholars and professionals.

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Student Organization
summary: CEGSO promotes social interaction between Chemical Engineering (chemE) graduate students and provides a forum for communication between the chemE graduate student body, departmental faculty and staff, and Clemson's Graduate Student Government.

name: Chi Omega
summary: An organization of young women dedicated to high purpose and helpfulness.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
summary: Our organization mainly focuses to provide support for Chinese students and professionals at Clemson University. We try our best to provide resources to help make their transition to Clemson smoother.

name: The Chronicle Magazine
summary: We are Clemson's student-run arts and literary magazine! We welcome students from all majors to come join us as we work to design, distribute, and promote our magazine and connect with the artistic community here at Clemson!

name: Clemson College Democrats
summary: Clemson College Democrats is a coalition of students who share similar liberal views on social justice and economic opportunity.

name: Clemson Engineers for Developing Communities
summary: Clemson Engineers for Developing Communities (CEDC) is a student-led service-learning organization, experiential-learning organization, and research opportunity. We are "serving the developing world, developing those who serve."

name: Clemson Society for Industrial-Organizational Psychology
summary: The Clemson Society for Industrial-Organizational Psychology is a professional student organization for Clemson's I-O Psychology graduate program as well as for interested graduate and undergraduate students.

name: Clemson University College of Arts and Humanities Ambassadors
summary: This organization is for the Ambassadors that represent the various majors in the College of Arts and Humanities.

name: Clemson University Microbiological Society
summary: The Clemson University Microbiological Society is an organization designed for undergraduate students with an interest in microbiology. You don't have to be a microbiology major to join!

name: Clemson University Rocket Engineering
summary: Our mission is to introduce the aerospace industry to Clemson University for all demographics, develop future leaders around the world, and research cutting-edge technology.

name: TigerLive Entertainment
summary: Clemson's premier student programming board delivering the biggest and best events to all Clemson students. Entertainment FOR students, BY students.

name: Cuong Nhu Martial Arts Club
summary: The Clemson Cuong Nhu Club is a student run martial arts and fitness organization at Clemson University. All Clemson students are eligible to join, regardless of previous experience or physical ability.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is an international professional business fraternity.

name: Engineers Without Borders
summary: We are an organization at Clemson University that seeks to solve problems in developing countries through engineering.

name: Environmental Engineering & Earth Sciences Graduate Student Organization
summary: This organization is for all graduate students who are interested in environmental engineering and earth sciences at Clemson. This was formerly known as Environmental Engineering & Earth Sciences Student Unification Committee Society.

name: Flying Tigers Booster Club
summary: To further the opportunities for cadets of Detachment 770

name: Formula SAE
summary: Clemson University's Formula SAE team is a non-profit student-run organization dedicated to designing and building an open-wheeled formula-style racecar in the Society of Automotive Engineers Student Design Competition: Formula SAE.

name: Material Advantage
summary: Material Advantage is an undergraduate MSE organization that allows its members to further engage with professors and network with professionals through various events. MA provides membership to four major national societies: ASM, TMS, AIST, and ACerS.

name: Clemson Offroad Club
summary: Jeeps, Toyotas and 4x4. No matter what it is, if it's got 4wd come wheel with us.

name: Omicron Delta Kappa
summary: Omicron Delta Kappa National Leadership Honor Society recognizes Clemson University students who are top leaders in scholarship, athletics, service, mass media and performing arts at the University and in the community.

name: Graduate Association of Materials Engineering and Science
summary: Formerly MRS and Optica, GAMES now represents all MSE graduate students. It’s a great way to meet peers, get involved in the department, and grow through networking and professional opportunities.

name: Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia - Sigma Mu Chapter
summary: Social Fraternity with a love for music.

name: Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity
summary: The Beta Kappa Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive national honor fraternity that welcomes eligible Clemson students the opportunity to join. We value a tripod of scholarship, leadership, and fellowship that guides our goal of social service.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
summary: Social fraternity comprised of members committed to academic excellence, campus involvement, and strong brotherhood.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is a mechanical engineering honor society that focuses on sound engineering ability, scholarship, service, leadership, and integrity.

name: Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics
summary: SIAM is an organization for students, professors, and professionals interested and involved in mathematics and its applications to the real world.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers - Clemson University Chapter
summary: Join the Familia! Our goal is to empower all student to develop their full academic and professional potential. We host Industry Night, professional workshops, social events, K-12 outreach, and attend SHPE National Convention.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: The Society of Women Engineers empowers women to achieve full potential as engineers and leaders, expands the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrates the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: Student Personnel Association
summary: SPA is a social and professional development organization for graduate students, faculty and staff within Student Affairs.

name: Student Veterans Association
summary: Welcome to the Clemson Student Veterans Association TigerQuest page!

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity
summary: Theta Tau is the oldest and largest co-ed professional fraternity for engineers. The Lambda Gamma chapter within Clemson University consists of 70+ active members who constantly strive for professional improvement while developing lifelong friendships.

name: Volunteers4LIFE
summary: Volunteers4LIFE strives to provide fun, genuine, and loving volunteers for the ClemsonLIFE program. We seek to empower LIFE students to reach their full potential and raise awareness for the program across Clemson's campus!

name: WSBF-FM Clemson
summary: WSBF-FM is Clemson University's student-run radio station.  Broadcasting on 88.1 FM and online through our website wsbf.net, we offer a wide variety of programming. WSBF also puts on live concerts throughout the semester.

name: Chi Sigma Alpha
summary: Chi Sigma Alpha honors and recognizes professionals, faculty, and graduate students in student affairs for excellence in three pillars: academics, service to the profession, and research.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: A group of students that have a passion for physics, math, computer science, and outreach.

name: Alpha Pi Mu
summary: Honor society for Industrial Engineering undergraduate and graduate students

name: Clemson Criminal Justice Club
summary: The Clemson Criminal Justice Club strives to improve criminal justice and foster professionalism in law enforcement. We promote professional, academic, and public awareness of criminal justice issues. We are an affiliate of the ACJA/LAE.

name: BANGLADESH ASSOCIATION CLEMSON
summary: BAC fosters communication, cooperation, and relationships between the Bangladeshi community and diverse cultures at Clemson University. It supports new Bangladeshi students and preserves cultural heritage through events and networking.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Sciences Honor Society
summary: TriBeta is an honorary society for students who are interested in improving the understanding and appreciation of study in biological sciences. In addition, the organization is committed to service and to increasing knowledge through scientific research.

name: Clemson Players
summary: The home of all things Theatre at Clemson University! The Clemson Players student organization is a theatrical community in which members may participate in various productions, professional development, social, and fundraising events.

name: Clemson Student Chapter of SHRM
summary: Our chapter seeks to prepare students for a professional career in HR by providing networking opportunities with professionals in the field.

name: Clemson UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund)
summary: Clemson UNICEF Campus Initiative engages with the local community to promote UNICEF's goal of child survival. We do this through campaigns, fundraisers, congressional advocacy, and volunteer work.

name: MBA Student Association
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to organize philanthropic, social and professional activities to MBA students not offered directly as part of the MBA program course-work, but critical to the overall benefit of the post-graduate experience.

name: Natural Resources Graduate Student Association
summary: NRGSA is committed to create, develop, and foster interaction between graduate students, staff, and faculty in different departments and disciplines within the field of natural resources.

name: Pershing Rifles Company C-4
summary: Pershing Rifles at Clemson develops students professionally, mentally and physically through discipline, drill, and other militaristic principles. We uphold Clemson's rich military history and promote the University's long standing traditions.

name: Psi Chi, The International Honor Society in Psychology
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: Clemson Event Planners Association
summary: Clemson Event Planners Association, or CEPA, is a group of students with career interests in events, hospitality, and marketing.

name: Tau Beta Pi
summary: Tau Beta Pi is the oldest national engineering society in the United States honoring engineering students who exemplify in academic achievement as well as character. A Tau Beta Pi member will also show a commitment to personal and professional integrity.

name: The Clemson Packaging Science Club
summary: Networking, hands-on experience, and education for students interested in the field of packaging science and related fields!

name: Agricultural Mechanization and Business Club
summary: Our club is designed for people within the Agricultural Mechanization and Business major and other majors who are seeking internship and job opportunities in the agricultural industry.

name: Clemson Amateur Radio Club
summary: We aim to encourage the science and art of amateur radio with Clemson students and the Clemson community through education, experimentation and friendship.

name: Gamma Epsilon Tau
summary: Gamma Epsilon Tau (GET) is the honorary/service organization for Graphic Communication Students. As a member of GET, you will have the opportunity to participate in workshops and department events and learn more about your major, faculty, and peers!

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
summary: IISE, the world's largest professional society dedicated solely to the support of the profession, is an international, nonprofit association that provides leadership for the application, education, training, research, and development of IEs.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: NSBE's mission is to "increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."

name: The Wildlife Society
summary: Our mission is to represent and serve the professional community of scientists, managers, educators, technicians, planners, and others who work actively to study, manage, and conserve wildlife and habitats worldwide.

name: Psi Upsilon
summary: United in friendship, Psi Upsilon members aspire to moral, intellectual and social excellence in themselves as they seek to inspire these values in society.

name: A.Bevy Collegiate Group
summary: Join us in sparking greater self-awareness! Our mission is to empower young adults with hope for a brighter future, the courage to chase their dreams, and the realization of their true purpose.

name: INFORMS Student Chapter at Clemson University
summary: INFORMS Student Chapter brings together people who share interests in Operations Research and Management Sciences.

name: Sister-2-Sister
summary: Sister-2-Sister seeks to positively impact the lives of its members through creating supportive networks among women of color, encouraging and assisting with personal and professional development, and providing strategies for educational success.

name: Clemson Economics Club
summary: The Clemson Economics Club is dedicated to providing students with a platform to explore, discuss, and deepen their understanding of economics.

name: Clemson University Tour Guides
summary: The Clemson University Tour Guides is a student organization whose members serve as University-sponsored tour guides. Guides are responsible for giving all campus tours through the Nieri Family Alumni and Visitors Center.

name: Clemson University Makerspace
summary: The Clemson Makerspace provides a safe, collaborative environment for students to work together on scientific, creative, or artistic endeavors. Currently the Makerspace provides students with access to 40 different 3D printers, 2 laser cutters, and more!

name: Genetics and Biochemistry Graduate Student Association
summary: The GBGSA pursues two goals. We desire to communicate and pursue the needs of graduate students with the departmental and college level administrations as well as develop community among graduate students.

name: Clemson Hope
summary: Clemson Hope is a non-profit organization dedicated to empowering students and teachers by meeting the needs of local Title 1 Elementary Schools. We partner with three local schools to provide mentoring programs and events for at-risk students.

name: College of Science Student Advisory Board
summary: We seek to improve the experiences of students in the COS through departmental, college, and university-wide initiatives. We select members as positions open up in each department and are open to grads and undergrads looking to make a positive change!

name: Student Chapter of the Institute of Management Accountants at Clemson University
summary: Institute of Managerial Accounting, introducing Accounting and Finance students into the world of Managerial Accounting and Manufacturing in the Corporate world.

name: Minority Business Student Association
summary: The Minority Business Student Association is dedicated to the professional, academic, and personal experiences of its members and the recruitment of minority business students into the College of Business.

name: Clemson Horticulture Club
summary: The Horticulture Club is dedicated to learning and teaching about the diverse realm of the horticultural sciences. It strives to broaden members' perspectives and experiences in the agricultural sciences as well as provide awareness.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: Electrical and Computer Engineering Honors Society

name: Clemson University Graduate Student Government
summary: The Graduate Student Government (GSG) is the official representative of Clemson University’s graduate student body. We are here to ensure that you have an amazing and memorable graduate student experience at Clemson University.

name: Black & Gold Club
summary: The Black & Gold Club is designed to serve as the primary booster club for Clemson University Army ROTC and provide opportunities for cadets in the realms of leadership, budgeting, and professional development.

name: Tigers PA Club - INACTIVE SP26
summary: Tigers PA Club is a student led organization for undergraduate students pursuing a career as a Physician Assistant.

name: Center for Student Leadership and Engagement
summary: We seek to create a uniquely Clemson experience that prepares every Clemson student to be a leader in their profession and community. We strive to prepare students to lead with honesty, integrity, and respect.

name: Marketing & Communications
summary: University Relations exists to protect and enhance the Clemson University brand by effectively and thoughtfully sharing what we know and love about Clemson University with the world.

name: Residential Living
summary: We shape residential environments to enhance learning and growth.

name: Staff Senate
summary: Staff Senate acts as a conduit between the University community and staff. The Senate is the recognized voice for staff. The Senate currently represents classified and unclassified full-time permanent, time limited, and temporary grant employees

name: Glenn Department of Civil Engineering
summary: Academic Department

name: Residential Learning
summary: Supporting student learning in residential communities.

name: Student Funding Board
summary: Student Funding Board is made up of students across Clemson University who are charged with the responsibility to allocate Student Activity Fee dollars by the Vice President of Student Affairs.

name: Biosystems Engineering Student Club
summary: The mission of BE Club is to provide social interaction for BE students between classes, professional networking opportunities, and community service opportunities in order to create a foundation for our members to make a more sustainable world.

name: CHANGE (Creating Habits and Norms Guiding Ethical Decisions)
summary: The mission of CHANGE is to inform and educate students of Clemson University about the importance of ethical decision-making and to encourage them to act as ethical leaders in their respective fields.

name: Women in Business
summary: The purpose of Women in Business is to facilitate events and programming focused on furthering women’s knowledge on how to succeed in the professional business world through speakers, workshops, panels, and more.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers Geo-Institute Graduate Student Organization
summary: The ASCE Geo-Institute Graduate Student Organization (GI-GSO) at Clemson University is established with the goal of enriching the geotechnical engineering study experience of the students.

name: Tigers for Green Innovation
summary: The Mission of Tigers for Green Innovation will be to create a greener, more sustainable Clemson University and Clemson community, through work in education and projects promoting on-campus innovation, including the Annual Sustainability Summit.

name: Chi Epsilon
summary: Chi Epsilon is the National Civil Engineering Honor Society in the United States, which honors students who exemplify the principles of “Scholarship, Character, Practicality, and Sociability” within the civil engineering profession.

name: Clemson Diplomacy Club
summary: Clemson Diplomacy Club (CDC) is an organization that unites undergraduate students interested in foreign affairs and international relations, specifically those who strive to hold occupations in diplomatic or non-governmental organization careers.

name: Environmental Engineering Club at Clemson University
summary: The EES Club is open to all undergraduate students who are environmental engineering majors or interested in similar topics. It is a place to connect with professionals and other environmental engineering students!

name: Clemson Residents in Science and Engineering
summary: Residents in Science and Engineering (RISE) is a unique living-learning environment created for first-year students to excel in their chosen STEM discipline at Clemson University.

name: Bridge to Clemson
summary: The Bridge to Clemson program is a dedicated transfer pathway from Tri-County Technical College to Clemson University.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Organization
summary: The Chemistry Graduate Student Organization (CGSO) serves as the representative body for graduate students in the Department of Chemistry at Clemson University.

name: American Mathematical Society
summary: AMS is a graduate student organization in the School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences.   We seek to serve graduate students by organizing professional development events as well as some social events.

name: Clemson Votes
summary: Focusing on broad-based, non-partisan efforts aimed at improving the voter engagement of our student population - graduate and undergraduate.

name: Eta Sigma Gamma
summary: The mission of Eta Sigma Gamma is promotion of the discipline by elevating the standards, ideals, competence and ethics of professionally prepared individuals in Health Education.

name: Clemson Association for Women in Mathematics
summary: The Clemson Association for Women in Mathematics (AWM) encourages those, particularly women, to pursue education and careers in fields related to the mathematical sciences by mentoring those at all stages of their mathematical career.

name: Experimental Forest Trail Runners
summary: The Experimental Forest Trail Runners exists to introduce students to trail running through free races and weekly trail runs in Clemson’s Experimental Forest. We accept all levels of runners regardless of ability!

name: College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences
summary: Our mission in the College of Engineering, Computing and Applied Sciences is to create future graduates who can be productive in a global economy.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: The American Society of Civil Engineers represents  members of the civil engineering profession. ASCE is the nation’s oldest engineering society.

name: Plant and Environmental Sciences Graduate Student Association
summary: The PES GSA provides social and professional development opportunities to foster meaningful relationships between PES graduate students, faculty, and staff on campus and the Research and Education Centers of Clemson University

name: College of Business Recruiting Ambassadors
summary: COBRA provides information sessions to prospective families and represents the college in many different ways.

name: Adobe Digital Studio
summary: A creative environment dedicated to providing Clemson with digital media resources and support.

name: Clemson Reel Dialogue
summary: Reel Dialogue is both Clemson's best club for people who love to watch and discuss movies and Clemson's only undergraduate journal dedicated to film studies.

name: Clemson Chick-fil-A Club
summary: The Clemson Chick-Fil-A club exists to educate students on the professional and philanthropic aspects of the Chick-Fil-A brand and to promote community among Clemson students as they enjoy Chick-Fil-A meals at each meeting.

name: Transfer Academic Programs
summary: The office of Transfer Programs collaborates with campus partners to provide academic and social programs to support new transfer students as they transition to Clemson.

name: Clemson University Honors College
summary: The Honors College is a community of over 1,600 students who want to make the most of their academic and personal experiences at Clemson. Through small class sizes, sponsored programs, mentoring, and networking, you can create your ideal education.

name: Bioengineering Student Advisory Council
summary: The Student Advisory Council for the Department of Bioengineering represent the greater Bioengineering student body to provide a source of internal feedback to the department.

name: Clemson Artificial Intelligence Club
summary: A platform for students of all educational backgrounds to get involved in the growing community around AI at Clemson University and across the world.

name: Clemson Society of Future Actuaries
summary: The Clemson Society of Future Actuaries (CSFA) serves as a hub for any Clemson students who are in the actuarial science program at Clemson, or are interested in becoming an actuary.

name: Edisto Research Association
summary: Organization for graduate students who are stationed at Clemson University - Edisto Research and Education Center, Blackville for the duration of their program.

name: Clemson Autism Advocacy and Inclusion
summary: CAAI members play a key role in educating the Clemson Family on ways to support positive change within the autism community. This is accomplished by raising awareness about current autism policy issues, to create a higher standard for inclusion.

name: General Engineering Advising Center
summary: The General Engineering Advising center serves first-year Freshman and Transfer students who intend to pursue engineering degrees.

name: Clemson Semper Fi Society
summary: The Clemson Semper Fi Society exists to connect Clemson students who wish to commission in the US Marine Corps after graduation. CSFS organizes training events for Marine Corps candidates in order to prepare them for success as Marine officers.

name: Marketing Student Advisory Board
summary: We serve to better the marketing department by serving as a voice for students and finding creative solutions with the Marketing Department faculty. We send a monthly newsletter and plan various trips to help enhance the marketing student experience.

name: Clemson Indian Students Association
summary:
We are an organization mainly focusing on Indian Students.
We help all Indian Students after they land in the USA. 
Our faculty adviser is Dr.Rajendra Singh.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers
summary: NOBCChE is an organization aimed at ensuring that African Americans and other people of color are fully engaged in shaping the global scientific community.

name: The Aurantiaco
summary: The Aurantiaco is Clemson's journal for the humanities and social sciences. Authored, edited, and published exclusively by undergraduate students, it aims to create a space for students in these fields to publish their high-level academic work.

name: Rutland Institute for Ethics
summary: Founded in 2001 by Robert J. Rutland, the Rutland Institute for Ethics has been a major partner in helping Clemson University achieve its mission by fostering young, ethical leaders on campus and beyond.

name: English Club
summary: The English Club is dedicated to increasing the literary presence on campus through creative projects, career resources, and community.

name: Lyceum Fellows Program
summary: At the Clemson Institute for the Study of Capitalism (CISC), our mission is to explore the moral foundations of capitalism.

name: The Women's Network
summary: Revolutionizing networking for collegiate and recently graduated women. Creating a community of women that celebrates the desire to achieve, cultivating ambition to prepare the next generation of leaders.

name: Summer Start
summary: Summer Start provides a select group of first-year students with an early opportunity to begin their Clemson academic journey.

name: Clemson's Club of Neurodiversity
summary: Clemson's Club of Neurodiversity is an organization dedicated to the enrichment of those who are neurodivergent within the Clemson Community. We are also a huge refugee from who those who suffer from anxiety, depression, etc.

name: Clemson Battle Buddies
summary: Battle Buddies is a nonprofit organization that raises, trains, and places service dogs with veterans suffering with PTSD. Members of this organization will help with fundraising and events. They may also decide to apply to be a volunteer Puppy Raiser!

name: Clemson University Teaching Fellows
summary: Teaching Fellows is a recruitment and development program in partnership between Clemson College of Education and CERRA (The Center for Educator Recruitment, Retention, and Advancement).

name: Tigers Against Cancer
summary: Tigers Against Cancer is a nonprofit organziation that is directly associated with the American Cancer Society. Our goal is to spread awareness about Cancer prevention and screening and to raise money for cancer research through fundraising events.

name: Clemson Aspiring Educators
summary: We strive to support, prepare, and empower diverse, aspiring educators with numerous resources, networks, and opportunities. Aspiring educators will be equipped to make a difference in their schools, communities, and in all phases of their careers!

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers Clemson University Student Chapter
summary: The Clemson ASHRAE student chapter seeks to better inform our members about the diverse opportunities in the HVAC industry and to connect with and collaborate with industry professionals to encourage future growth in this field.

name: Clemson Cyber
summary: Clemson's Cybersecurity student organization

name: Gift of Life Marrow Registry
summary: Headed by campus ambassadors for the national organization Gift of Life, the purpose of this group is to add members of the Clemson campus to the bone marrow and stem cell donation registry, to help patients find their perfect match.

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
summary: The Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society is a professional development and social organization affiliated with the nationally recognized American Chemical Society. We focus on hosting outreach and social events, and attending conferences.

name: Clemson University Police Department
summary: CUPD works closely with students, faculty, and staff to create a safe living, learning, and research environment on the Clemson campuses. We work to provide professional, courteous, and effective law enforcement service to our community.

name: Pee Dee Graduate Student Association
summary: The Pee Dee Graduate Student Association was established to provide students located at the Pee Dee Research and Education Center with a strong sense of community while also allowing students opportunities for professional development and volunteering.

name: Clemson Undergraduate Real Estate Club
summary: Clemson Undergraduate Real Estate Club serves to facilitate events to expose students to different aspects of the real estate industry. The club's mission is to provide members with access to knowledge about the industry from experienced speakers.

name: Research Troubleshooting Club
summary: A student lead forum that discusses technical problems in members' research with the aim to solve those problems and producing a community that faces imposter syndrome head-on.

name: German Professional Society
summary: The German Professionals Society (GPS) at Clemson University is an initiative aimed at promoting interest in German language, culture, and professional development. This club seeks to create a vibrant community for students who are passionate about Germa

name: Graduate Student Advisory Council of School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences
summary: GSAC is an organization that collaborates with the department to enhance the experience of graduate life, solves valid issues with the consultation of the ADGS, and increases communication between the graduate body and Clemson University.

name: Transfer 2 Tiger
summary: The Transfer2Tiger Peer Mentor Program (T2T) is a peer mentoring program where mentees are matched with an upper-class transfer student (a mentor)  to help navigate their transition to Clemson.

name: Association for Supply Chain Management
summary: An organization for supply chain and operations majors to gain internship/work opportunities, network with people in the field, listen to guest speakers and learn more about the supply chain industry as a whole.

name: Clemson University Design, Build, Fly
summary: Design, Build, Fly is an engineering design team that competes in AIAA Design, Build, Fly competition. The competition is to design, build, and fly an RC airplane that best meets the challenge rules of the current year.

name: Clemson Scholars of Finance
summary: Scholars of Finance is a non-profit organization that aims to inspire character and integrity in the next generation of financial leaders.

name: Women in Economics
summary: Women in Economics is designed to support women majoring or interested in the economics field, specifically by providing tools on how to network and empowering all women to reach their personal goals despite the large gender gap the economic field faces.

name: Society of Doctoral Students in Automotive Engineering
summary: This organization aims to promote a supportive and inclusive community for doctoral students in Automotive Engineering that fosters camaraderie, helps build relationships, and bolsters professional growth through symbiotic collaborations.

name: Clemson Build
summary: Stop action faking. Start building. Build is an ecosystem for doers. We trade generic networking for "Vibe Code" sessions, pitch roasts, and cross-club collabs. We provide the pressure to perform and the community to support. Don't burn alone.

name: College of Engineering, Computing, and Applied Sciences Graduate Student Advisory Board
summary: The CECAS Graduate Student Advisory Board exists to encourage and advocate for graduate students and their well-being while being a bridge of communication to administration at Clemson.

name: Women in Mechanical and Automotive Engineering
summary: A group focused on retention and recruitment of women in engineering, achieved through building a network of community members, providing critical mental and academic support, preparing for future success and establishing life-long relationships.

name: Clemson Kappa Alpha Pi - Pre Law & Government Fraternity
summary: Clemson KAPi, Kappa Alpha Pi, is a gender-inclusive pre-law and government professional fraternity. Within the Sigma Chapter, we welcome undergraduates passionate about law school and/or government service, irrespective of their field of study.

name: Planning Student Organization at Clemson
summary: Planning Student Organization at Clemson is a student-led organization which promotes networking and professional growth in the city, urban, and regional planning profession.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha: Eta Lambda
summary: An invite-only national Political Science Honors Society, whose local chapter values giving back to the Clemson Community through scholarship and service.

name: Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student Organization
summary: PandA GSO aims to build solidarity among the Physics and Astronomy Graduate Student body by planning social and professional development events and by working with faculty to better the department.

name: American Water Works Association - Water Environment Federation Student Chapter
summary: This organization connects students to professionals in the water/wastewater/environmental industries. This is done through events such as career panels, guest speakers, and local outreach (lake cleanup).

name: Nintendo Club
summary: We host free to attend casual gaming tournaments, in person meetings and hang outs to discuss our shared interest, Nintendo Direct watch parties, and even more fun events!

name: Clemson Libraries
summary: Clemson Libraries has six locations, more than 3 million print and digital collections items, and a dedicated team of library professionals who can help you with your research assignments and other projects.

name: Social Media Listening Center
summary: An interdisciplinary lab built to harvest, analyze, and interpret social and digital media.

name: Clemson Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant Club
summary: This club will provide students who are pursuing a career as an anesthesiologist assistant to become part of a community of like-minded individuals, network with AA programs and anesthesia providers, and learn more about the profession

name: College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board
summary: The mission of the College of Education Graduate Student Advisory Board (GSAB) is to promote the welfare of the graduate student body, to enhance relationships between students and faculty, and to serve as ambassadors for the College of Education.

name: Financial Management Association
summary: Our mission is to provide a fully integrated program that combines education, career preparation, industry intellectual capital, and alumni engagement to prepare students for careers in finance.

name: American Society of Biomechanics Student Chapter at Clemson University
summary: It is a Clemson University's American Society of Biomechanics (ASB) student chapter.

name: STEM Start
summary: STEM Start is for students that have been admitted to the Summer II STEM Start Program.

name: People Advancing Women's Empowerment
summary: PAWE is a new club on campus, which stands for People Advancing Women's Empowerment! It is a gender-inclusive club that will be hosting our ongoing speaker series, networking, and professional development events.

name: Catan Club
summary: Join us in exploring the exciting world of Settlers of Catan! Whether you’re a seasoned Catan strategist or a curious newbie, our club offers a welcoming space to engage in friendly competition, master new strategies, and share your passion!

name: Clemson Cars and Coffee
summary: Clemson Cars and Coffee is designed to bring together car enthusiasts from within the university and the local community to host car gatherings and meets.

name: Clemson University Constructors Guild
summary: An umbrella organization for all the clubs with the Construction Science Department. The Guild serves as a liaison between the faculty/industry and the students.

name: Clemson University Literacy Leaders Student Association
summary: The Literacy Leadership Student Association is dedicated to empowering undergraduate and graduate students across all programs by providing access to essential literacy resources and information.

name: Clemson Students for Mental Health
summary: Clemson students come together as we do fun activities to release mental stress, as well as outreach programs and education to end mental health stigma. "Keep looking where the light pours in" -Morgan Harper Nicoles.

name: Kappa Delta Pi
summary: Kappa Delta Pi's national mission is to "inspire and equip teachers to thrive." This organization is focused on those who plan to work in the education field and how to support them in college and beyond and give back to the community.

name: Clemson Pre-Law Litigation Club
summary: The objectives of the Clemson Pre-Law Litigation Club are to develop a foundational understanding of litigation, business, and economics through hands-on experiences, educational workshops, and engagement with industry professionals.

name: Clemson Mycology Club
summary: The Clemson Mycology Club explores fungal diversity through hands-on activities, outdoor discovery, and educational events. Members learn from faculty and national speakers, promote environmental stewardship, and engage with fungi beyond the classroom.

name: Clemson Cure Club
summary: Clemson Cure Club is a non-profit organization funding pediatric cancer patients and their families. Through fundraising and various events, we provide college students with the opportunity to take part in CURE and make an impact on local patients.

name: Clemson Finance Club
summary: Assisting in the recruitment of Clemson’s financial management majors into full-time jobs after graduation. Our club invites employers to visit Clemson for informational sessions about various positions at their companies.

name: Division of Research
summary: The Division of Research supports researchers at Clemson. We assist faculty in pursuing competitive research funding, managing awards, navigating complex regulation and compliance matters, collaborating with industry, and commercializing technologies.

name: Critical Research Initiative
summary: The Critical Research Initiative focuses on in service learning for incarcerated individuals residing in correctional facilities nearby the Clemson campus.  This organization also investigates and presents research on the overall topic of incarceration.

name: Tension Release Engineered Ballistics
summary: Tension Release Engineered Ballistics is a club team built specifically for Engineering and Physics students who are looking for hands on experience with projectile motion machines and want to get familiar with working in teams to complete projects.

name: Paw Packs
summary: By utilizing our members at Paw Packs we will collect and distribute various care packages to local homeless shelters surrounding the Clemson community.

name: Tiger Shock
summary: Raising funds for lifesaving AEDs in local schools. Empowering safety, one heartbeat at a time.

name: Clemson Quantum Club
summary: We want to give students the opportunity to learn and interact with quantum computing with no assumptions of prior knowledge. We encourage participation across disciplines, and want to provide tools for hands-on learning.

name: Athletics Marketing and Fan Experience
summary: Clemson Athletics Marketing and Fan Experience brings energy, creativity, and passion to build strong and loyal fan bases and aims to deliver gamed experiences that are unmatched!

name: FeelGood Clemson
summary: FeelGood isn’t just a club, we’re a movement of passionate college students taking action with purpose. We are hosting bold events, and raising critical funds for The Hunger Project’s mission to end hunger. The FeelGood program framework reflects the fou

name: Clemson Law Review
summary: The Clemson Law Review (CLR) is a student-edited journal dedicated to publishing high-quality legal scholarship on pressing topics.

name: Healing Harmonies
summary: Healing Harmonies aims to bring hope and healing through the power of live music in hospitals and healthcare settings. Beyond service, we also seek to advance knowledge through research into music's influence on pain perception, stress, and well-being.

name: Health Horizons Journal
summary: This organization aims to address and combat global health challenges through knowledge, innovation, and research translation through collaboration with students and professionals globally, through research publications and multimedia outreach.

name: Clemson Renewable Energy Club
summary: Clemson Renewable Energy Club is the best way to get hands on engineering expierience with renewable energy source mechanisms. Join student lead teams to research, model, design, and build renewable and sustainable sources of energy.

name: Botanical Society of America Clemson Chapter
summary: The Botanical Society of America Clemson Chapter connects plant lovers through plant-related events and offers discounts on BSA memberships, Botany conference fees, and research awards.

name: Clemson Speech Language Pathology Association
summary: The Clemson Speech-Language Pathology Association (CSLPA) is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting education, awareness, and professional development in the field of Speech-Language Pathology.

name: BICEFS Graduate Student Association
summary: The Baruch Institute of Coastal Ecology and Forest Science (BICEFS) Graduate Student Association was established to bring together off-campus students utilizing this REC.

name: Clemson Financial Planning Club
summary: The Clemson Financial Planning Club connects students with industry leaders, hosts recruiting and networking events, and explores the CFP® pathway and financial planning career opportunities.
